sql.title: SQL for beginners
sql.description: Learn SQL from the ground up with beginner-friendly lessons on databases, SELECT, WHERE, sorting, and practical querying
sql.moreComingSoon: More SQL lessons are coming soon.

sql_module_0.title: Module 0 — Intro to SQL
sql_module_0.description: Understand SQL, databases, and how organized data is stored in tables.
outcomes: Explain what SQL stands for
Describe what a database is
Distinguish a database from a table
Recognize rows, columns, and records
Explain why SQL is useful in real systems
why: Builds the mental model before writing queries
Makes SELECT, FROM, WHERE, and later joins feel more natural

sql_module_1.title: Module 1 — Reading Data with SELECT
sql_module_1.description: Learn how to read data from tables with SELECT and understand the basic shape of SQL queries.
outcomes: Use SELECT to read data from a table
Select all columns or specific columns
Recognize the basic structure of a SELECT query
Practice simple beginner table-reading queries
why: SELECT is the foundation for almost everything learners do first in SQL
Reading data clearly is the first step before filtering, sorting, and grouping

sql_module_2.title: Module 2 — Filtering Data with WHERE
sql_module_2.description: Learn how to narrow results with WHERE, compare values correctly, combine conditions safely, and practice filtering with real beginner SQL queries.
outcomes: Use WHERE to narrow results correctly
Compare text and numeric values using SQL operators
Combine logical conditions without changing the intended meaning
why: Filtering is how learners move from seeing all data to seeing useful data
It builds the decision-making habit needed for realistic querying

sql_module_3.title: Module 3 — Sorting and Limiting Results
sql_module_3.description: Learn how to sort rows in a meaningful order and keep only the rows you need.
outcomes: Sort rows with ORDER BY
Sort by more than one column
Use LIMIT to control result size
Combine ORDER BY and LIMIT for top-N style questions
why: Sorting helps learners make result tables easier to read
Limiting helps focus answers and supports practical top/bottom queries

sql_module_4.title: Module 4 — Working with Text, Lists, Ranges, and NULL
sql_module_4.description: Learn how to search partial text, filter with lists and ranges, and handle missing values safely in real-world SQL queries.
outcomes: Match partial text with LIKE patterns
Use % and _ wildcards correctly
Filter values with IN and NOT IN
Filter ranges with BETWEEN
Handle missing data with IS NULL and IS NOT NULL
why: Real datasets often contain inconsistent text, grouped choices, and missing values
These filtering tools make SQL much more useful for realistic search and cleanup tasks

section_0_1.title: Module 0 — Introduction to SQL
section_0_1.description: A beginner introduction to SQL, databases, tables, rows, and columns.
bullets: How tables, rows, and columns work
Why SQL is useful in real systems

section_1_1.title: Module 1 — Reading Data with SELECT
section_1_1.description: Learn how SELECT reads data from tables and how to choose which columns to return.

section_2_1.title: Module 2 — Filtering Data with WHERE
section_2_1.description: Learn why filtering matters, how WHERE works, how comparisons behave, and how to combine conditions safely.

section_3_1.title: Module 3 — Sorting and Limiting Results
section_3_1.description: Learn how to control result order and result size with ORDER BY and LIMIT.

section_4_1.title: Module 4 — Working with Text, Lists, Ranges, and NULL
section_4_1.description: Learn how to match partial text with LIKE, filter lists and ranges with IN and BETWEEN, and handle missing values safely with NULL checks.
